purpose: Define reasoning chains that explain why handoff context judgment holds.
load_condition: Load when the user asks why, or when tradeoffs in handoff decisions matter.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Decision loss is the most expensive form of information loss because decisions constrain all future work.
logic: Every decision made during a session establishes a constraint: a path chosen, an alternative rejected, a tradeoff accepted.
These constraints shape all subsequent work. Changing a decision requires undoing everything built on it.
If the decision is lost in handoff, the next agent may make a different decision, creating contradictory constraints.
Contradictory constraints produce code that works locally but fails systemically.
Therefore, preserving decisions (and their rationale) is the highest-priority handoff content.
reasoning_chains.so_what: Always include why a decision was made, not just what was decided. The rationale enables the next agent to evaluate whether the decision still holds.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Signal-to-noise ratio determines handoff effectiveness because attention is the scarce resource.
logic: The next agent has limited attention for the handoff document.
Every piece of noise (derivable information) consumes attention without adding value.
As noise increases, the probability that the next agent misses signal approaches certainty.
A concise handoff with high signal-to-noise ratio is more effective than a comprehensive one with low ratio.
Therefore, removing noise is not optional — it is essential for the handoff to function.
reasoning_chains.so_what: If the next agent can derive it from code, docs, or git, don't include it. Every word in the handoff must earn its place.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Forward-looking context is necessary because the next agent needs direction, not just history.
logic: A handoff without next steps is a historical document, not an operational one.
The next agent must decide what to work on first. Without direction, they may start with the wrong thing.
Risks and warnings prevent the next agent from falling into known traps.
Priority ordering tells the next agent what matters most, enabling them to make tradeoffs if time is limited.
Therefore, the handoff must be both backward-looking (context) and forward-looking (direction).
reasoning_chains.so_what: End every handoff with: what to do next, what to watch out for, and what matters most.
